FAQ

## Still have questions? Good founders do.

How the brain works, what the agents can touch, and what happens to your data.

What exactly does Agently do?

It builds a company brain across your tools, then runs agents that act on it: draft, send, update, report. It isn't a chatbot. The work happens in the background and lands as real output you can ship.

  Which tools does it connect to?

Over 100 through MCP connectors: Slack, Linear, Notion, Google Drive, Stripe, HubSpot, GitHub, Gmail, and the rest of your stack. Two-way, OAuth, live updates.

  Do agents act on their own, or do I approve?

results.title: Why are AI agents frustrating? Exosphere solves the problem. | Nikita Agarwal posted on the topic | LinkedIn
results.content: Why is everyone (including me) crazy about AI agents today? Agency. But if agents are taking away from your agency instead of multiplying it, what's the point? Spending hours setting them up. Spending hours refining the prompts. Spending hours deploying them. What's the point of agents then? -'I would rather do it myself!' Which is why everyone is (borderline) frustrated by how agents are performing today. We need proof of true agency. And for such proof, agents need STRUCTURE. To reliably build trust. They still need someone to hold them and instruct which pathways to take and avoid. That's Exosphere. An AI workflow engine that can run smallest tasks to create largest workflows, up and running in under 30 mins with your custom code. With validations, structure, and the flexibility to [...] AI agents are mostly bullsh\t. I've built 70+ of them here are 3 that actually work. MIT just released a report: 95% of AI projects fail. After deploying dozens of these systems, I know exactly why. Everyone's chasing flashy agent "swarms" instead of building things that work. Companies fall for complex multi-agent systems that sound great in demos but break in real life. Here's what happens: one agent makes stuff up, and your entire system fails. AI will confidently lie rather than say "I don't know." (I've watched a client's "revolutionary" 10-agent system crash because we got caught up in the hype.) The real damage?  Burned budgets with zero results  Stakeholders who lose trust in AI  Teams wasting months on systems that never work  Joining that 95% failure rate But three [...] code. With validations, structure, and the flexibility to take new paths as you go. So that agents can deliver trust, reliably. Dreaming of what can be achieved, goosebumps!

results.title: Agently Review: Pricing, Features, Pros & Cons
results.content:
Where Agently stands out

 Structured onboarding, training, and coaching for agents and small teams
 Built-in accountability and activity tracking to reinforce productive habits
 Goal setting and progress dashboards to keep pipelines moving
 Handy marketing aids (social templates, video scripts) to speed up content creation
 AI-assisted listing tools to package and promote listings faster

Where it may fall short

 Less compelling for larger organizations that need deep, enterprise-grade integrations and complex workflows
 Lighter marketing automation than full-scale CRMs and all-in-one suites
 Public pricing/packaging is less transparent than some competitors [...] You’ll also find built-ins like broker-to-agent messaging, an events calendar, and real-time leaderboards. If what you need is a paid lead flow within a CRM, consider the alternatives listed above. If you want to develop agents and drive production habits, Agently is a great fit!

Yes! Agently is a web-based platform you can access through your mobile or tablet. Its clean interface is user-friendly and easy to set up.

Absolutely! With just one click, you can easily sync your CRM to Agently, enhancing its functionality with existing workflows. This integration feature allows agents to leverage Agently’s full potential alongside their preferred software.

## Bottom line: Is Agently worth your money? [...] SHARE
